title: GAIA execution-evidence.json — qa-review test execution evidence
description: Test execution evidence captured by /gaia-review-qa via review-common/qa-test-runner.sh and consumed by verdict-resolver.sh through --execution-evidence. Records per-suite outcome, timing, and the resolved tier/context so a downstream verdict can apply the precedence rules.

suites.description: Per-suite execution evidence array. Empty when skipped=true.

name.description: Suite identifier — usually the tier key (tier_1, tier_2, tier_3) or the bridge-supplied suite name.

command.description: The command line that was executed.

exit_code.description: Process exit status. 0 = pass; non-zero = fail; 124 = timeout (POSIX timeout convention).

duration_seconds.description: Wall-clock time the suite ran (seconds, may be fractional).

pass_count.description: Number of passing tests, when extractable from suite output. Zero when not extractable.

fail_count.description: Number of failing tests, when extractable from suite output. Zero when not extractable.

coverage_delta.description: Optional coverage delta produced by review-common/action/coverage-delta.sh.

timeout.description: True when the suite was killed for exceeding timeout_seconds.

required.description: True when the suite is required for the active tier — drives verdict-resolver precedence.

skip_reason.description: Populated when the suite was skipped (e.g., placement does not match active context).

tier.description: The single tier name when one tier ran, the literal 'multi' when more than one tier ran, or 'none' when execution was skipped.

context.description: Resolved GAIA_EXECUTION_CONTEXT used to filter tier placements.

wall_clock_seconds.description: Total wall-clock time spent inside qa-test-runner.sh (seconds, fractional).

skipped.description: True when test_execution was absent or no tier matched the active context.

bridge_used.description: True when execution was delegated to the Test Execution Bridge.

diagnostics.description: Free-form diagnostic strings emitted during execution (e.g., INFO 'test_execution not configured').
